title: Week of 22 June
intro: Mid-week update: built from what we already bought, with the first few days logged as eaten. Leaning into what the kids actually like — Grandma's creamy carrots & potatoes. (Also had broccoli with miso dip and a radish–cucumber salad earlier in the week.)

days.dish: Oyster mushrooms in a Japanese-style sauce with rice
days.core: Parents: oyster mushrooms simmered in a soy–ginger sauce, served over rice.
days.kids: Potatoes with quark, and champignons with cheese — both largely rejected this time.

days.dish: Millet (Hirse) with white bean paste, roasted pumpkin & zucchini
days.core: Parents: millet with a white-bean paste alongside roasted pumpkin and zucchini.

days.dish: Aubergine & chickpea tomato stew with spaghetti
days.note: Kids out this evening — just the two of you.
days.core: Sauté onion, garlic and ginger, add diced aubergine, baby tomatoes and the jar of chickpeas, and simmer into a soft stew. Serve over spaghetti or rice.

Sweat finely chopped onion in olive oil until glassy. Add diced carrots and potatoes and let them take on a little colour, then add a little vegetable broth dissolved in water. In a second pot, melt butter, stir in ½–1 Tbsp flour and cook briefly, then whisk in soy milk (or milk) to a sauce consistency; season with a little Maggi, paprika and a pinch of nutmeg. Fold the sauce through the vegetables. Quick version: stir a little flour into cold water, add to the veg with milk/soy and spices, and simmer to a sauce. Serve as is or with rice.
days.kids: This is the one they like — keep it simple, just carrots & potatoes.
days.dairy: Stir a little cream or quark into yours.
days.toddler: Ideal for the little one — soft, mild, low salt; go easy on the Maggi.

days.core: Sauté shimeji and champignons with garlic and ginger, season with soy sauce and a touch of sweetness, and serve over rice.
days.toddler: Chop the mushrooms small (they're chewy) and keep it mild.

days.core: Indian-style onion–tomato masala with cumin, coriander and turmeric; add the second aubergine and potatoes and cook until soft. Serve with rice.
Same creamy flour + soy/milk sauce as Thursday, this time with the last of the mushrooms and carrots, tossed through pasta.
days.kids: They like creamy — finish with a little cheese.

vesper.note: Older one's snack box, 5 days a week. A bit bigger on Tuesday and Thursday. Savoury only — no sweet muffins etc.

days.idea: Carrot & cucumber sticks, bread with butter & honey-margarine, a few almonds.
Pretzel with Wurst & margarine, mini tomato halves, boiled egg slices, a small handful of cashews.
Bread with Aufstrich, carrot sticks, mini tomato halves, a few almonds.
Bread roll with Wurst & butter, boiled egg slices, carrot & cucumber sticks, baby tomatoes, cashews.
Pretzel–Wurst–margarine, carrot sticks, mini tomato halves, a few cashews.

items: Maggi, paprika, nutmeg, cumin, coriander, turmeric

shoptitle: If you want to top up
shopnote: Optional extras to round out the week and the Vesper box — only grab what you need. Tap to check off.

items: Bell peppers — 2 (Vesper strips + stir into the rice bowl)
Fresh coriander — 1 bunch (Thu stew + Sat sabzi)
Baby tomatoes — 1 extra punnet (going fast across dinners + Vesper)
Firm tofu — 1 block (vegan protein for Friday's bowl)
Cheese — small piece (kid add-on + your dairy)
Bread or rolls + pretzels (Brezel) — for the Vesper box
Fish fillet — 1–2 (optional Fisch option for the kids)
Coconut milk — 1 tin (to enrich a curry without cream, keeps it vegan)

preptip: The creamy flour-and-milk/soy sauce (Grandma's method) is the backbone twice this week — Wednesday's carrots & potatoes and Sunday's mushroom pasta. Make a little extra on Wednesday and keep it for Sunday. Boil a few eggs at the start of the week for both dinners and the Vesper box.

title: Week of 29 June
intro: Built from your own recipe app this week — tofu katsu, a mild Japanese curry and the crispy miso tofu tacos — with a clear kid path spelled out for every night. The dishes are vegan at the core; the kids' version is usually just the crispy bit served plain, before the heat goes in.

days.note: Mild and sweet — the whole-family anchor for the week.
days.core: Sauté onion, carrot and potato with a little garlic and ginger. Add water and a mild Japanese curry roux (or the japanese curry bullion), and simmer until the veg is soft and the sauce is thick and glossy. Stir in peas at the end. Serve over rice.
days.kids: Kid-friendly as it is — serve over rice. Add a fish fillet or boiled egg on the side if they want more.
days.toddler: Soft veg, mild roux, low salt — mash lightly into the rice.

days.dish: Tofu katsu with rice & a cucumber-cabbage salad
days.core: Coat tofu filets in flour, then a quick oat-milk wash, then panko, and shallow-fry until golden. Serve with rice, a crunchy cucumber and cabbage salad, and tonkatsu sauce.
days.kids: A reliable win — give them plain katsu strips, or with a little ketchup, plus rice. Hold the tonkatsu sauce if they prefer.
days.toddler: Cut the katsu into soft strips with the sauce on the side; chop the raw salad very small or skip it.

days.note: From your recipe app — the tofu sticks double as the kids' dinner.
days.core: Crumb tofu sticks (cornstarch, then oat-milk, then panko seasoned with smoked paprika and cumin) and shallow-fry. Simmer the miso-BBQ — soy sauce, red miso, apple cider vinegar, agave, tomato paste and a splash of water — until thick. Make a pico of tomato, red onion, corn, coriander and lime. Build tacos with avocado.
days.kids: The panko tofu sticks ARE the kid meal — serve plain sticks with avocado and a warm tortilla; hold the miso-BBQ and the oniony pico.
days.toddler: Tofu sticks cut small with mashed avocado in a soft tortilla — no chilli, no raw onion.

days.dish: Cashew tofu stir-fry with seasonal veg & rice
days.core: Stir-fry tofu until golden, then add broccoli, bell pepper and green beans with garlic, ginger and spring onion. Toss through a soy-sesame stir-fry sauce with cashews. Serve over rice.
days.kids: Pull a plain portion before the chilli goes in — tofu, cashews and rice.
days.toddler: Keep it mild; grind or finely chop the cashews (whole nuts are a choking hazard) and cut the veg small.

days.dish: Somen noodles with a sesame-soy dip & steamed greens
days.core: Cook somen and rinse cold. Serve with a light sesame-soy dipping sauce (a little mirin in it) and quick-steamed greens like spinach or pak choi.
days.kids: Plain cold noodles are a kid favourite — mild dip on the side.
days.toddler: Cut the noodles short; keep the dip mild and low-salt.

days.note: Weekend fry-up from your recipe app.
days.core: Toss cauliflower florets in a cornstarch batter and fry until crisp. Make the manchurian sauce — soy sauce, tomato paste, a little gochujang or chilli, ginger, garlic and spring onion — and toss the gobi through. Serve with rice or noodles.
days.kids: Set aside plain fried florets before the spicy sauce, or toss theirs in a mild soy glaze.
days.toddler: Soft florets cut small; skip the spicy sauce.

days.core: Sweat finely chopped onion, add diced carrots and potatoes and a little vegetable broth. Make the creamy flour and oat-milk sauce, season gently with a little Maggi, paprika and nutmeg, and fold it through the veg. Serve as is or with rice.
days.kids: The one they actually like — keep it simple, just carrots & potatoes.
days.dairy: Stir a little cream or quark into yours.
days.toddler: Soft, mild and low salt — ideal for the little one.

breakfasts: Oatmeal or Seitenbacher müsli with oat milk and strawberries or blueberries
Toast with peanut/almond butter or vegan Nutella + banana
Alpro vanilla yoghurt with fruit and a few cashews
Weekend: jumbo chickpea pancake (savoury — from your recipe app)
lunches: Leftover curry or katsu with rice
Somen or rice bowl with veg

vesper.note: Older one's snack box, 5 days a week. A bit bigger on Tuesday and Thursday. Savoury only.

days.idea: Carrot & cucumber sticks, bread with Aufstrich, a few almonds.
Pretzel with Wurst & margarine, mini tomato halves, boiled egg slices, a small handful of cashews.
Bread with vegan cheese, cucumber rounds, mini tomato halves.
Roll with Wurst & butter, boiled egg slices, carrot & cucumber sticks, baby tomatoes, cashews.
Pretzel, Wurst & margarine, carrot sticks, a few cashews.

shopnote: Pantry staples are assumed in stock — this is the fresh produce, a tofu top-up and the kids' add-ons. Tap to check off.

items: Carrots — ~1 kg (Mon curry, Thu stir-fry, Sun creamy pot)
Large tomatoes — 2, plus 1 punnet baby tomatoes (Wed pico + Vesper)
Garlic — 1 bulb; fresh ginger — 1 thumb (most nights)
Spinach or pak choi — 1 bag (Fri greens)
Tofu — top up to ~4 blocks/filets (katsu, tacos and stir-fry go through a lot)
Eggs — 6 (boiled-egg Vesper + optional with the curry)
Fish fillets — 1-2 (optional side for the kids, e.g. with the curry)

pantry: Soy sauce, red miso, mirin, sesame oil
Apple cider vinegar, agave, tomato paste
Oat milk, cashews, tortillas, tonkatsu sauce, flour
preptip: Tuesday's katsu, Wednesday's tacos and Saturday's gobi all share the same crumb-and-fry station — double the tofu on Tuesday and stash sticks for Wednesday's tacos. Boil a batch of eggs at the start of the week for the Vesper box. Both the Japanese curry and Sunday's creamy carrots reheat well, so make extra.
